author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Felix the Martyr, truly Felix, i.e. “Happy” both in his name and his crown, whose birthday this is, despised the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was he, because he feared the Lord, thence happy, thence blessed, because his wife was as a fruitful vine upon the earth, and his children stood around his table?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these blessings he hath perfectly, but in the Body of Him who is here described; and, because he understood them in this sense, he scorned things present, that he might receive things future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye are aware, brethren, that he suffered not the death that other martyrs suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he confessed, and was set aside for torments; on another day his body was discovered lifeless.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They had closed the prison to his body, not to his spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The executioners found him gone; when they were preparing to torture, they spent their rage for nought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was lying dead, without sense to them, that he might not be tortured; with sense with God, that he might be crowned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence was he also happy, brethren, not only in name, but in the reward of everlasting life, if he loved these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed are all they that fear the Lord, and walk in His ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He speaketh to many; but since these many are one in Christ, in the next words he speaketh in the singular: “For thou shalt eat the labours of thy fruits.”…When I speak of Christians in the plural, I understand one in the One Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye are therefore many, and ye are one; we are many, and we are one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are we many, and yet one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because we cling unto Him whose members we are; and since our Head is in heaven, that His members may follow.…Let us therefore so hear this Psalm, as considering it to be spoken of Christ: and all of us who cling unto the Body of Christ, and have been made members of Christ, walk in the ways of the Lord; and let us fear the Lord with a chaste fear, with a fear that abideth for ever.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt eat the labours of thy fruits” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And ye, O thou, ye many who are One, “Thou shalt eat of the labours of thy fruits.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seemeth to speak perversely to those who understand not: for he should have said, thou shalt eat the fruit of thy labours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many eat the fruit of their labours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They labour in the vineyard; they eat not the toil itself; but what ariseth from their labour they eat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They labour about trees that bear fruit: who would eat labours?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the fruit of these labours, the produce of these trees; it is this that delighteth the husbandman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “Thou shalt eat the labours of thy fruits”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At present we have toils: the fruits will come afterwards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since their labours themselves are not without joy, on account of the hope whereof we have a little before spoken, “Rejoicing in hope, patient in tribulation;” at present those very labours delight us, and make us joyful in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore our toil has been what could be eaten, and could also delight us; what will be the fruit of our labour when eaten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They who went weeping on their way, scattering their seed,” did eat their labours; with how much greater pleasure will they eat the fruits of their labours, who “shall come again with joy, bearing their sheaves with them”?…“Blessed art thou, and well shall it be with thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed art thou,” is of the present: “well shall it be with thee,” is of the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou eatest the labours of thy fruits, “blessed art thou;” when thou hast reached the fruit of thy labours, “well shall it be with thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if it be well with thee, thou wilt be happy: and if thou wilt be happy, thou wilt also have all well with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there is a difference between hope and attainment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If hope be so sweet, how much sweeter will reality be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us now come to the words, “Thy wife” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): it is said unto Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His wife, therefore, is the Church: His Church, His wife, we ourselves are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As a fruitful vineyard.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in whom is the vineyard fruitful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we see many barren ones entering those walls; we see that many intemperate, usurious persons, slave dealers, enter these walls, and such as resort to fortune-tellers, go to enchanters and enchantresses when they have a headache.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this the fruitfulness of the vine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this the fecundity of the wife?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are thorns, but the vineyard is not everywhere thorny.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath a certain fruitfulness, and is a fruitful vine; but in whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon the sides of thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not all are called the sides of the house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I ask what are the sides.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall I say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are they walls, strong stones, as it were?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he were speaking of this bodily tenement, we should perhaps understand this by sides.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We mean by the sides of the house, those who cling unto Christ.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wife and the children are the same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In these carnal marriages and wedlocks, the wife is one, the children other: in the Church, she who is the wife, is the children also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostles belonged to the Church, and were among the members of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were therefore in His wife, and were His wife according to their own portion which they held in His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then is it said concerning them, “When the Bridegroom shall be taken from them, then shall the children of the Bridegroom fast”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She who is the wife, then, is the children also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I speak a wonderful thing, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the words of the Lord, we find the Church to be both His brethren, and His sisters, and His mother.…For Mary was among the sides of His House, and His relatives coming of the kindred of the Virgin Mary, who believed on Him, were among the sides of His House; not in respect of their carnal consanguinity, but inasmuch as they heard the Word of God, and obeyed it.…He added; “For whosoever shall do the will of My Father which is in heaven, the same is My brother, and sister, and mother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Brother,” perhaps, on account of the male sex whom the Church hath: “sister,” on account of the women whom Christ hath here in His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “mother,” save that Christ Himself is in those Christians, whom the Church daily bringeth forth Christians through baptism?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In those therefore in whom thou understandest the wife, in them thou understandest the mother, in them the children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6. …Such children ought therefore to be “around” the Lord’s “table, like olive-branches.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A complete Vine it is, a great bliss: who would now refuse to be there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou seest any blasphemer have a wife, children, grandchildren, and thyself perchance without them, envy them not; discern that the promise hath been fulfilled in thee also, but spiritually.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore we have, why have we?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because we fear the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lo, thus shall the man be blessed that feareth the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is the man, who is also the men; and the men are one man; because many are one, because Christ is One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord from out of Sion bless thee: and mayest thou see thee good things that are of Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even to the birds was it said, “Be fruitful and multiply.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to hold as a great blessing what was given unto birds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can be ignorant, that it was given indeed by the voice of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But use these goods, if thou receive them; and rather think how thou mayest nourish those who have been born, than that others may be born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not happiness to have children, but to have good ones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Labour in the task of nourishing them, if they be born; but if they be not born, give thanks unto God.…Thy children are infants: thou dost caress the infants: the infants caress thee: do they abide thus?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou wishest they may grow, thou wishest that their age may increase.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider that when one age cometh, another dieth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When boyhood cometh, infancy dieth; when youth cometh, boyhood dieth: when manhood cometh, youth dieth; when old age cometh, manhood dieth: when death cometh, all age dieth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As many successions of ages as thou wishest for, so many deaths of ages dost thou wish for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things therefore “are” not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, are children born unto thee to share life with thee on earth, or rather to shut thee out and to succeed thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoicest thou in those born to exclude thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Boys when born speak somewhat like this to their parents: “Now then, begin to think of removing hence, let us too play our parts on the stage.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the whole life of temptation in the human race is a stage play; for it is said, “Every man living is altogether vanity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, if we rejoice in children who will succeed us; how much must we rejoice in children with whom we shall remain, and in that Father for whom we are born, who will not die, but that we may evermore live with Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the good things of Jerusalem: for they “are.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how long shall I see the good things of Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All thy life long.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thy life be for ever, thou wilt see the good things of Jerusalem for evermore.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “if in this life only,” saith the Apostle, “we have hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what reason were the Martyrs condemned to beasts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can it be declared? by what means, or what tongue can tell it? or what ears can hear it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That indeed, “Neither ear hath heard, nor hath it entered into man’s heart:” only let us love, only let us grow in grace: ye see, then, that battles are not wanting, and that we fight with our lusts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We fight outwardly with unbelieving and disobedient men; we fight inwardly with carnal suggestions and perturbations: we everywhere as yet fight.…What sort of peace then is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One from Jerusalem, for Jerusalem is interpreted, A vision of Peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then “mayest thou see the good things that are of Jerusalem,” and that, “all thy life long—and mayest thou see,” not only thy children, but, “thy children’s children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, Thy children?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy works which thou here dost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are thy children’s children?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fruits of thy works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou givest alms: these are thy children: for the sake of thine alms thou receivest everlasting life, these are thy children’s children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mayest thou see thy children’s children;” and there shall be “peace upon Israel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6), the last words of the Psalm.…
